source: Original plain-English definitions written by the project author.

terms.MACRS: Modified Accelerated Cost Recovery System. The depreciation method most U.S. business assets use for tax. Bundled percentage tables (Pub 946) prescribe the deduction by class life and convention.
terms.FICA: Federal Insurance Contributions Act. The combined Social Security (6.2%) and Medicare (1.45%) tax. Self-employed pay both halves via Schedule SE; employees split it with the employer.
terms.Section_179: An election to expense the full cost of qualifying business property in the placed-in-service year, up to an annual cap that phases out above a property-cost threshold.
terms.bonus_depreciation: An additional first-year depreciation under IRC 168(k). The percentage phases down (80% in 2023 down to 20% in 2026 and 0% in 2027 unless Congress extends).
terms.DSO: Days Sales Outstanding. Average number of days between making a sale and collecting cash on it.
terms.DIO: Days Inventory Outstanding. Average number of days inventory sits before being sold.
terms.DPO: Days Payable Outstanding. Average number of days between receiving a supplier invoice and paying it.
terms.contribution_margin: Sale price per unit minus variable cost per unit. The dollars each unit contributes toward fixed costs and profit.
terms.statute_of_limitations: The deadline by which a lawsuit must be filed. After it runs, the claim is barred regardless of merit. Each state and claim type has its own period and accrual rule.
terms.jurisdictional_maximum: The largest dollar amount a court is allowed to award. Small-claims courts have low maxima ($6k - $25k by state).
terms.ABC_test: A worker is an independent contractor only if all three prongs are met: free from control, outside the usual course of business, engaged in an independently established trade.
terms.FLSA: Fair Labor Standards Act, 29 USC 201+. Sets the federal minimum wage, overtime (1.5x over 40 hr/wk), and tip-credit framework.
terms.molarity: Concentration in moles of solute per liter of solution. M = mol / L. The standard chemistry concentration unit.
terms.RCF: Relative Centrifugal Force, expressed as multiples of gravitational acceleration g. Also called g-force.
terms.pKa: The pH at which an acid is half-dissociated. Buffers work best within ~1 pH unit of their pKa.
terms.hemocytometer: A glass slide with a precisely etched grid (improved Neubauer) used to count cells under a microscope. Each large square holds exactly 0.1 microliter.
terms.RPM: Revolutions Per Minute. The rotational speed of a centrifuge rotor.
terms.C1V1_C2V2: The dilution identity: stock concentration times stock volume equals final concentration times final volume.
terms.IUPAC: International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry. Maintains the standard atomic weights table cited per element in the bundled molecular-weight calculator.
terms.GHS: Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labelling of Chemicals. Provides the pictograms and signal words used on chemical containers.
terms.Wayfair_economic_nexus: Post-South Dakota v. Wayfair (2018), states can require remote sellers to collect sales tax once they cross a sales or transactions threshold, even without physical presence.
